title: Conversation-to-ticket extractor (Passive Skill 15)
depends_on: T-110 (streaming turn log reader; reused for tailing turns.jsonl)
current_state: Fixes and decisions discussed in chat regularly fail to get ticketed unless the user explicitly requests it. This session alone: privacy scrub, cost label fix, Telegram reconnect fix, image-analysis bug, skill upgrade — all surfaced in conversation and only became tickets after the user reminded the agent. ticket_candidate_miner (Skill 8) scans code + logs but not the conversation transcript itself.
structural_problem: Conversation is a first-class source of engineering signal but the passive skill suite ignores it. The longer a session, the more decisions get lost in chat history that never become tracked work.
target_state: scripts/passive/conversation_ticket_miner.py (Skill 15) reads the last 24h of logs/turns.jsonl via the streaming reader, asks Groq llama-3.3-70b to extract any (fix, decision, recurring complaint, new feature idea) that does not match an existing ticket title in tickets/open|closed. Writes candidates to analysis/conversation_candidates.jsonl. Wired into passive_daily_digest as Skill 15.
audit_refs: Phase 8.8 retro — session of 2026-05-24 produced 5 tickets after-the-fact
design_notes: Reuse agent.turn_log._tail_jsonl for streaming the last 24h window
Filter: only turns with role=assistant and decisions/fixes language (kept lightweight — Groq does the heavy lifting)

Prompt: 'Below is a conversation excerpt. Extract any (a) fixes that were applied, (b) decisions about future work, (c) recurring user complaints. For each, propose a ticket title and 1-line description. Format JSON.'
Dedup against existing titles via simple cosine on title strings (threshold 0.7 to catch rewordings)
Threshold logic: PASS = 0 candidates, WARN = 1-3, FAIL = 4+ (encourages action when backlog grows)
Same Status.value convention as other passive skills
success_criteria: Run after a session that discussed 3 fixes → writes 3 candidate entries to JSONL
Candidates that match existing ticket titles (cosine > 0.7) are filtered out
risk_notes: False positives from casual chat ('we could maybe X') would inflate WARN/FAIL. Mitigation: tighten prompt to require explicit fix/decision language. Cost: Groq free tier; one call per skill run.
migration_plan: 1. Write conversation_ticket_miner.py using memory_pollution_detector.py as template
2. Add Groq client init with .env auto-load (mirror agent/skill_triage.py pattern)
4. Tests: synthetic turns.jsonl with known fixes; assert candidates extracted
